authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Plumas National Forest

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cub Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cub Lake not listed)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Lakes of the Lakes Basin — lakesbasin.com
sources.quotedText: Cub Lake is along the Bear Lakes Loop. It is three quarters of a mile from the trailhead. It is at about 6600 feet.

sources.title: Lakes Basin Loop: Long Lake, Cub Lake, Little & Big Bear Lake — California Through My Lens
sources.quotedText: you will see Cub Lake, the smallest of the lakes on this hike. As the name states, this is a small lake, and while it is still beautiful, it pales in comparison to the others.

sources.title: Fishing the Lakes Basin Recreation Area — California Fly Fisher
sources.quotedText: The lakes include Goose Lake, Haven Lake, Round Lake, Big Bear Lake, Little Bear Lake, Cub Lake, Silver Lake...

parameters.note: Cub Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Cub Lake is a small lake accessible only by trail — about three-quarters of a mile from the trailhead along the Bear Lakes Loop in the Lakes Basin Recreation Area, Plumas National Forest, at roughly 6,600 feet elevation. It is described as the smallest of the lakes on the Lakes Basin Loop hike. No public CDFW stocking record was found for Cub Lake specifically.

regulations.citation: lakesbasin.com — Lakes of the Lakes Basin; California Through My Lens — Lakes Basin Loop Hike
regulations.humanSummary: Small lake reached by a short trail (~0.75 mi) from the trailhead on the Bear Lakes Loop; part of the Lakes Basin Recreation Area and described as the smallest lake on the Lakes Basin Loop hike
regulations.verbatimText: Cub Lake is along the Bear Lakes Loop. It is three quarters of a mile from the trailhead. It is at about 6600 feet.
